name: Puffy Omelet Squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the zucchini by cutting up and slicing (1/2 cup).
2. Spray an 8x8x2-inch baking dish with Pam, set aside.
3. For the omelet mixture, beat egg yolks, onion powder, salt, and 1/8 tsp pepper for about 4 minutes or until thick and lemon colored; set aside.
4. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form (tips fall over); fold into egg yolks.
5. Spread egg mixture evenly into prepared dish.
6. Bake in a 350°F oven for 22-25 minutes, or until a knife inserted near the center comes out clean.
7. Meanwhile, prepare sauce by combining undrained tomatoes, zucchini, and 1/8 tsp pepper in a saucepan. Bring to boiling; reduce heat.
8. Cover and simmer about 5 minutes, or until zucchini is tender. Simmer, uncovered, for an additional 10-12 minutes, or to desired consistency.
9. To serve, cut omelet into quarters; top with sauce.
Per serving: 144 calories, 10 g protein, 7 g carbohydrates, 9 g fat, 411 mg cholesterol, 395 mg sodium, 392 mg potassium.
